name: The Union Bank Company
description_short: The Union Bank Co. is an FDIC-insured community bank headquartered in Columbus Grove, Ohio, offering personal and business banking services including checking, savings, loans, and merchant services.
description_long:
The Union Bank Company is a traditional community bank based in Columbus Grove, Ohio, operating under NMLS 433131 and maintaining FDIC insurance backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The bank emphasizes local service with their tagline "WHERE LOCAL STILL MEANS SOMETHING," positioning themselves as an alternative to larger national institutions.

The Union Bank offers comprehensive banking products for both personal and business customers. Personal services include checking and savings accounts, CDs/IRAs, mortgage loans, home equity and personal loans, credit cards, and wealth management. They provide digital banking through online platforms and a mobile app, plus Zelle money transfer services. Business offerings include commercial checking and savings, commercial loans, SBA and government programs, business credit cards, treasury management, online banking, merchant services, and their proprietary Re$ubmitIt check resubmission service. The bank has implemented extended ATM hours (7am-7pm weekdays, 9am-12pm Saturday) staffed by Virtual Tellers for customer convenience.

The Union Bank distinguishes itself through community focus and personalized service. They offer a $200 welcome bonus on Rewards Checking accounts and maintain a Financial University platform providing financial education to customers. Their extended Virtual Teller hours at ATMs (Interactive Teller Machines) and live chat support differentiate them from competitors with limited service hours. The bank actively communicates security awareness through scam alerts and educates customers about fraud prevention.

As a community bank, The Union Bank serves regional customers rather than maintaining national branch networks, which limits accessibility for consumers outside Ohio. Their website provides basic product information but lacks detailed rate comparisons, fee schedules, or in-depth product specifications that would help prospective customers evaluate competitiveness. The bank positions itself appropriately as a local alternative for community-oriented banking needs.
services: Personal checking accounts with rewards options
Mortgage loans for home purchase and refinance
Home equity loans and personal loans
Commercial loans and SBA/government program financing
Business credit cards and treasury management
Merchant services and credit card acceptance
Online and mobile banking with Zelle integration
Virtual Teller ATM services with extended hours
Financial education through Financial University platform
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Extended Virtual Teller hours at ATMs (7am-7pm weekdays, 9am-12pm Saturday) for after-hours banking
Comprehensive product suite including personal and business banking, mortgages, SBA loans, and merchant services
$200 welcome bonus available on Rewards Checking accounts
Free Financial University platform offering financial education to customers and communities
Zelle money transfer integration for peer-to-peer payments
Live chat support available during business hours for customer assistance
Community-focused philosophy with local decision-making rather than corporate bureaucracy
cons: Limited geographic availability as a regional Ohio-based bank, not accessible nationwide
Website lacks detailed fee schedules, interest rates, and competitive product comparisons
No specific mention of minimum account balances, eligibility requirements, or product specifications
Virtual Teller service limited to ATM channels rather than video teller at physical locations during extended hours
Limited information about credit card rewards programs, mortgage rates, or loan terms on public website
best_for: Ohio residents and businesses seeking personalized, community-focused banking relationships
Small business owners and agricultural operators needing SBA loans and specialized services like merchant processing
Customers prioritizing local bank service and decision-making over national chain convenience
Individuals wanting FDIC-insured deposits with extended digital access outside traditional banking hours
diagnosis: The Union Bank Co. is best for Ohio-based consumers and business owners seeking relationship-driven community banking with FDIC protection and extended digital access. The main caveat is geographic limitation—this is a regional bank without national presence, so customers outside Ohio or those requiring broad branch networks should look elsewhere.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
